meta_findings.title: FALSE POSITIVE CONSENSUS: All agents hallucinated dead code
meta_findings.description: All 6 agents reported dead code at lines 322-340 in _is_free_or_subscription function. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The function actually ends at line 318 with 'return False'. Line 320 starts a NEW function '_get_cost_category'. There is NO dead code.

evidence.actual_code:
Line 318: 'return False' (end of _is_free_or_subscription)
Line 320: 'def _get_cost_category(model: ModelInfo) -> str:' (new function starts)
evidence.verification: Read tool verified lines 280-350 of display.py. No duplicate code exists.
evidence.root_cause: Agents likely hallucinated based on similar-looking code patterns without verifying actual line numbers.

meta_findings.resolution: DISMISS ALL 6 findings as false positives. This is a systematic validation failure across all agents.

meta_findings.title: MISSED: Global mutable state without concurrency protection
meta_findings.description: All agents missed the critical concurrency issue: module-level global variable '_cache' (line 88) is modified without thread safety. In multi-terminal environments, concurrent cache reads/writes can cause data corruption.
meta_findings.evidence: display.py:88 (_cache = None), display.py:97,153 (global _cache statements). No locks protect concurrent access.
meta_findings.impact: Race conditions in multi-terminal environments. Concurrent cache operations can corrupt data or cause crashes.
meta_findings.recommendation: Add file-based locking (use filelock package) around cache operations.
meta_findings.why_missed: Agents focused on code style issues and missed the architectural concurrency vulnerability. Only code-critic (CC-20260322-002) identified this, but with MEDIUM severity instead of CRITICAL.

meta_findings.title: MISSED: sys.path manipulation violates python-2025 standards
meta_findings.description: Line 22 manually inserts CSF_SRC into sys.path, violating python-2025 standards which prohibit manual sys.path manipulation.
meta_findings.impact: Import system fragility. Violates project coding standards.
meta_findings.recommendation: Install CSF as editable package or set PYTHONPATH environment variable.
meta_findings.why_missed: Agents focused on duplicate imports (lines 25-26) but missed the more serious sys.path manipulation violation. Only code-critic (CC-20260322-004) identified this.

meta_findings.title: MISSED: Silent exception masking in cache operations
meta_findings.description: Cache load/save operations catch all Exception types and only print dim messages, making failures invisible to users.
meta_findings.impact: Cache failures are silent. Users cannot distinguish between 'no cache' and 'cache error'.
meta_findings.recommendation: Log cache errors at warning level or raise critical errors.
meta_findings.why_missed: Security agent (SEC-004) identified broad exception handling but categorized as LOW severity and pre-existing. The actual impact on debuggability is higher.

meta_findings.description: Inflated severity for local file issues. SEC-001 (MEDIUM) for cache deserialization is overstated - this is a local cache file, not user input. SEC-003 (MEDIUM) for file permissions is irrelevant for single-user Claude Code environment.
meta_findings.recommendation: Calibrate severity to actual threat model. Local cache files in single-user environment don't need MEDIUM severity security validation.

meta_findings.description: PERF-005 reports 'Minor String Building Inefficiency' but concludes 'No action required' because current code is already efficient. This is not a real finding.
meta_findings.recommendation: Filter out non-findings before reporting. Don't report issues that resolve to 'no action needed'.

meta_findings.description: COMP-002, COMP-003 focus on duplicate import statements (trivial style issue) while missing the more serious sys.path manipulation violation (line 22).
meta_findings.recommendation: Prioritize findings by actual impact, not line count. sys.path manipulation is a standards violation; duplicate imports are noise.

meta_findings.description: All 6 agents reported the SAME false positive (dead code at lines 322-340). This suggests either: (1) agents are copying each other's findings, or (2) there's a systematic flaw in static analysis that mis-identifies function boundaries.
meta_findings.recommendation: Add verification step before reporting findings. Read the actual code lines to confirm the issue exists before reporting.

meta_findings.title: Severity contradiction for TOCTOU race condition

agent_a.name: adversarial-performance
agent_a.description: TOCTOU Race Condition in Cache File Operations

meta_findings.resolution: CRITICAL is correct. Multi-terminal TOCTOU can cause cache corruption and crashes. This is a high-impact concurrency issue.

meta_findings.title: Category contradiction for arbitrary timeout

agent_a.name: adversarial-compliance
agent_a.description: Magic number without named constant for timeout

agent_b.name: code-critic
agent_b.description: Arbitrary Timeout Value - violates reasoning_flaws principle

meta_findings.resolution: Both are correct, but code-critic's framing is stronger. 'Arbitrary threshold' is a reasoning flaw, not just style. The 30.0 value lacks evidence-based justification.

meta_findings.description: Finding reports dead code at lines 322-340. This code does not exist. Function ends at line 318. Agent hallucinated the issue.
meta_findings.recommendation: Implement verification step. Read actual code lines before reporting. Confidence should be 0 for non-existent issues.

meta_findings.description: Finding reports dead code at lines 322-340. This code does not exist. Same hallucination as SEC-002.
meta_findings.recommendation: Same as SEC-002. Verify findings against actual code before reporting.

meta_findings.recommendation: Same as SEC-002. Verify findings against actual code before reporting.

meta_findings.recommendation: Same as SEC-002. Verify findings against actual code before reporting.

meta_findings.description: Finding reports dead code at lines 322-340. This code does not exist. Same hallucination as other agents. Confidence of 100 is wildly inappropriate for non-existent issue.
meta_findings.recommendation: Same as SEC-002. Verify findings against actual code before reporting. 100 confidence for non-existent issue is a critical calibration failure.

critical_fix_before_merge.issue: TOCTOU Race Condition in Cache File Operations
critical_fix_before_merge.reason: Multi-terminal cache corruption risk. Confirmed valid finding.

critical_fix_before_merge.issue: Global mutable state without concurrency protection
critical_fix_before_merge.reason: All agents missed this. Thread-safety violation for _cache global variable.

high_fix_before_merge.issue: Timeout Risk: API Fetches Exceed 30s Timeout
high_fix_before_merge.reason: 30s timeout will be exceeded under p99 latency. Confirmed valid finding.
Standards violation. Only code-critic identified, missed by others.

medium_fix_before_merge.reason: Silent failures reduce debuggability. Confirmed valid finding.

medium_fix_before_merge.issue: Silent exception masking in cache operations
medium_fix_before_merge.reason: Cache errors invisible to users. Impact on debuggability.

dismiss_false_positives.reason: CODE DOES NOT EXIST. Systematic hallucination across all 6 agents.
Finding itself says 'No action required'. Not a real issue.

dismiss_false_positives.issue: Unsafe deserialization of cached model data
dismiss_false_positives.reason: MEDIUM severity is inflated for local cache file in single-user environment. Actual risk is LOW.

dismiss_false_positives.issue: Missing file permission checks on cache file operations
dismiss_false_positives.reason: MEDIUM severity is irrelevant for single-user Claude Code environment. Actual risk is NONE.

agent_accuracy.adversarial-compliance: 0% valid (0/5 findings - all trivial or false)

systematic_issues: GROUPTHINK HALLUCINATION: All 6 agents reported same non-existent dead code issue
VERIFICATION FAILURE: No agent verified findings by reading actual code lines
SEVERITY INFLATION: Security agent over-escalated local file issues to MEDIUM
FOCUS MISALLOCATION: Compliance agent focused on trivial duplicate imports while missing serious sys.path violation
BLIND SPOT: All agents missed critical concurrency vulnerability in global _cache variable
recommendations: Add mandatory verification step: All agents must Read() actual code lines before reporting findings
Implement finding deduplication: Detect when multiple agents report same issue
Calibrate severity to threat model: Local cache files ≠ user input
Prioritize by impact: Concurrency > standards > style
Add false positive penalty: Agents with >30% false positive rate need recalibration
